quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: An LLM tournament ranks candidates on rationale, pharmacology and literature quality, selecting which candidates advance.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Concordance with expert preferences and intra-rater consistency constitute an agreement study for this ranking channel.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Physical assay results are analyzed and routed back to drive the next round of candidate generation.

quotes.quote: Given a disease, Robin automatically identifies relevant in vitro assays that model key disease mechanisms and proposes specific drug candidates to evaluate in these experimental models.
quotes.rationale: Establishes that the evaluated evidence is produced in in vitro experimental models.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Analysis of assay data is split between human preprocessing and an automated component, making the substrate hybrid.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: A distinct human-performed analysis of experimental readouts, with no stated routing of its output.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Confirms human involvement in processing sequencing data prior to automated analysis.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: The system's outputs are not executable protocols and no validator of protocol executability is described.

diagnostics.consequence: Validator autonomy is overstated unless human execution and analysis steps are coded separately; the physical-assay feedback path is only partially machine-closed.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.statement: Human researchers perform all physical experimentation and part of the data analysis, so the iterative loop depends on unautomated human execution and preprocessing steps.

diagnostics.consequence: Agreement with experts cannot be upgraded to probabilistic calibration or to evidence that top-ranked candidates succeed in assays.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

diagnostics.statement: Reported reliability evidence for the LLM judge is expert agreement plus intra-rater consistency, not calibration or outcome-grounded validation against experimental results.

diagnostics.consequence: Executability is coded as not_reported rather than validated, and human translation remains a required bridging step.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

diagnostics.statement: No validator of protocol executability is reported; the system produces experimental outlines rather than executable protocols.

diagnostics.consequence: Cross-round state retention and control of iteration cannot be coded; memory and policy facets remain not_reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

diagnostics.statement: Memory structure and policy-level human authority are not described in the packet despite multi-round operation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report an outcome-grounded validation linking LLM-judge tournament ranks to measured in vitro assay results, separately from the existing expert-agreement study.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Top-ranked versus lower-ranked hit rate difference with a reported statistical test
success_check.evidence_needed: Per-candidate ranks paired with physical assay readouts and a predictive-validity analysis
success_check.observable: Association between judge rank and assay outcome for ranked candidates

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.

recommendations.proposed_action: Document explicitly which loop steps are human-performed versus automated, including experiment execution, sequencing preprocessing and flow cytometry analysis, and state where each human output is routed.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported loop step has an assigned actor and a stated downstream destination
success_check.evidence_needed: Workflow description or run logs identifying actor and routing for each step
success_check.observable: A per-step role table covering generator, executor, validator, memory and policy for each round

exact_quotes.exact_quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add and report an explicit executability check on generated experimental outlines before they are handed to laboratory staff.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Measured and reported against the current outline-only baseline
success_check.evidence_needed: Protocol-level execution records noting whether human translation was required
success_check.observable: Fraction of generated protocols executed in the lab without human rewriting

policy_type: LLM-driven iterative hypothesis generation and drug candidate proposal
LLM-judged tournament ranking for candidate selection
